channel.title: Libertas - Freedom Tech Signals
channel.description: Curated insights on freedom technology: Bitcoin, privacy tools, censorship resistance, and digital sovereignty.

item.title: Age Verification Systems Create Privacy and Surveillance Risks as Regulations Spread
item.description: New age verification laws worldwide are forcing platforms to implement identity-linked systems that threaten anonymous internet access and create surveillance infrastructure.

item.title: Access Now: 2025 Saw Record Internet Shutdowns, 30 in 15 African Countries
item.description: Access Now's #KeepItOn coalition documented 313 internet shutdowns globally in 2025, including 30 shutdowns across 15 African countries, with protests and elections as primary triggers.

item.title: Age Verification Laws Fragment Internet Access Across US, UK Implementation Models
item.description: Age verification laws are creating fragmented internet access as platforms withdraw from states with requirements, while different enforcement models emerge globally with significant privacy implications.

item.title: EFF: Google & Amazon Ignore Human Rights Risks in Israeli Military AI Contract
item.description: EFF reports Google and Amazon continue Project Nimbus cloud/AI services to Israeli military despite internal warnings and documented human rights risks, while refusing transparency or meaningful action.

item.title: EFF Calls for Action Against Clean FISA Section 702 Reauthorization
item.description: EFF is mobilizing opposition to a 'clean' reauthorization of FISA Section 702, which allows warrantless surveillance of Americans' communications with foreign persons overseas.

item.title: Iran Reportedly Accepts Bitcoin for Strait of Hormuz Transit Tolls
item.description: Iran is reportedly allowing ships to pay transit tolls through the Strait of Hormuz using Bitcoin and cryptocurrency, potentially bypassing traditional financial sanctions.

item.title: China Human Rights Lawyer Yu Wensheng Expected Release After 3-Year Detention
item.description: Human rights lawyer Yu Wensheng faces release April 13 after serving 3 years for 'inciting subversion' - part of systematic targeting of legal advocates challenging state power.

item.title: Citizen Lab Warns of Digital Transnational Repression 'Tsunami' Facing Canadians
item.description: Citizen Lab director testified to Parliament that digital transnational repression will intensify due to U.S. authoritarianism, AI expansion, and Canada's failure to regulate mercenary spyware as promised.

item.title: US Bans All Foreign-Made Consumer Routers, Requires FCC Approval
item.description: US now requires FCC approval for all foreign-made routers, citing supply chain vulnerabilities and cybersecurity risks. Companies must disclose foreign investors and plan US manufacturing to get approval.

item.title: FCC Chair Uses License Threats to Enforce Pro-Trump Editorial Control Over Broadcasters
item.description: FCC Chair Brendan Carr systematically threatens broadcast licenses to suppress coverage critical of Trump, targeting national stories while claiming to protect local interests.

item.title: Reddit Subpoenaed by Grand Jury to Unmask Anonymous Anti-ICE User
item.description: Federal prosecutors are using a grand jury to force Reddit to reveal personal data of an anonymous user who criticized Trump's immigration crackdown, marking dangerous escalation in government attacks on anonymous speech.

item.title: Ugandan Journalist Disappeared After Discussing Struck-Down Surveillance Laws
item.description: Journalist Mukose Arnold Anthony was forcibly disappeared by plainclothes security after discussing Constitutional Court ruling that nullified Computer Misuse Act sections used to target activists.

item.title: Proton Warns Age Verification Laws Create New Privacy Risks for Children
item.description: Proton argues that government age verification laws are creating dangerous privacy vulnerabilities, citing Discord's recent breach of 70K government IDs collected under UK regulations.

item.title: UAE Criminalizes Citizen Documentation of Drone Strikes, Arrests 135+ People
item.description: UAE authorities arrested 135+ people for filming/sharing footage of Iranian drone strikes, threatening 2+ years imprisonment and $55K fines for contradicting official narratives.

item.title: Amazon Uses Computer Crime Laws to Block AI Price Comparison Tool
item.description: Amazon successfully sued Perplexity's AI browser Comet under CFAA for helping users compare prices, setting dangerous precedent that could criminalize research and competition.

item.title: FISA 702 Renewal Could Target VPN Users as 'Foreign' for Warrantless Surveillance
item.description: Section 702 renewal debate reveals VPN users may be classified as 'foreign' targets, subjecting Americans to warrantless surveillance simply for using privacy tools.

item.title: 42 Media Orgs Challenge Trump's Executive Order Sanctioning Law Firms
item.description: The Intercept's Press Freedom Defense Fund leads 42 media organizations filing amicus brief to block Trump's executive order that sanctions law firms representing his political opponents.

item.title: Afghan Women Use Underground Online Education Networks to Resist Taliban Restrictions
item.description: First-person account reveals how Afghan women are creating underground online education networks and digital resistance methods to circumvent Taliban's education bans since 2021.

item.title: EFF Highlights Police Surveillance Mission Creep in Latest Newsletter
item.description: EFF's latest newsletter examines how police surveillance tech sold for serious crimes gets repurposed for traffic enforcement and protest monitoring, plus NSA reform updates.

item.title: Access Now Releases 2025 Internet Shutdowns Report
item.description: Access Now published their annual KeepItOn report documenting global internet shutdowns in 2025, highlighting rising government repression and citizen resistance.

item.title: EFF Welcomes Daily Show Viewers, Highlights 35-Year Fight Against Digital Surveillance
item.description: The Electronic Frontier Foundation showcases its three-decade battle against government surveillance, from the Crypto Wars to NSA dragnet spying, while promoting Executive Director Cindy Cohn's new book.

item.title: Alabama City Prosecutes Protester for Penis Costume at Anti-Trump Rally
item.description: Fairhope, Alabama arrested and is prosecuting a 62-year-old protester for wearing an inflatable penis costume at a political rally, doubling down with additional charges despite viral backlash.

item.title: Iranian-American Women Officials Condemn Trump's War Threats Against Iran
item.description: Iranian-American women in elected office reject Trump's threats against Iran, arguing war undermines the 'Woman, Life, Freedom' movement and Iranian self-determination.

item.title: Somalia's New Northeastern State Detains Journalist for Criticizing President
item.description: Journalist Abdiqani Abdirahman Mohamed detained without charge since March 26 by Northeastern State authorities over video criticizing President Abdiqadir Ahmed Aw-Ali.

item.title: EFF Defeats Copyright Troll Attack on Activist Hosting Provider May First
item.description: EFF successfully defended May First Movement Technology against baseless copyright demands from Higbee & Associates, protecting critical hosting infrastructure for social justice groups worldwide.

item.title: EFF's Cindy Cohn on Daily Show to Discuss 30 Years of Privacy Advocacy
item.description: EFF Executive Director Cindy Cohn appears on The Daily Show to discuss her new book covering three decades fighting surveillance, from the Crypto Wars to NSA dragnet spying.

item.title: Citizen Lab Submits Anti-Spyware Recommendations to Canadian Parliament Committee
item.description: Citizen Lab recommended comprehensive spyware regulations to Canada's intelligence oversight committee, arguing government spyware use creates human rights and national security risks.

item.title: Treasury Secretary, SEC Chair Push Congress for Crypto Market Structure Bill
item.description: Treasury Secretary Bessent, SEC Chair Atkins, and former Crypto Czar Sacks urge immediate Senate passage of the Clarity Act to establish clear regulatory boundaries for crypto and prevent innovation flight overseas.

item.title: Nigerian Press Freedom Groups Document 231+ Journalist Attacks Under Tinubu Administration
item.description: Press freedom organizations formally challenge VP Shettima's claim of no journalist harassment in Nigeria, documenting 231 verified attacks since 2023 including detentions, assaults, and killings.

item.title: Russia Labels Press Freedom Org IWMF as 'Undesirable Organization'
item.description: Russia designated the International Women's Media Foundation as an 'undesirable organization,' banning its operations and criminalizing support with up to 6 years prison.

item.title: Russian Police Raid Novaya Gazeta, Detain Journalist on Data Privacy Charges
item.description: Russian authorities raided independent newspaper Novaya Gazeta's Moscow office and detained journalist Oleg Roldugin under criminal charges for allegedly misusing personal data in reporting.

item.title: CPJ Issues 10-Point Plan to Restore Press Freedom in Hungary Ahead of Election
item.description: Committee to Protect Journalists outlines steps to dismantle Hungary's sophisticated media capture system, including ending Pegasus surveillance of reporters and abolishing Russia-style 'foreign agent' laws.

item.title: US Rewrites Procurement Rules to Force AI Companies to Disable Safety Guardrails
item.description: New GSA procurement rules would require AI contractors to license systems for 'all lawful purposes' and disable safety guardrails that refuse government requests, effectively weaponizing procurement against AI safety measures.

item.title: EFF Promotes New Book on 30 Years of Digital Surveillance Resistance
item.description: Electronic Frontier Foundation's Executive Director Cindy Cohn published 'Privacy's Defender,' chronicling three decades of legal battles against government surveillance from the Crypto Wars to NSA dragnet spying.

item.title: EU Parliament Blocks Mass Chat Scanning, But 'Chat Control' Proposal Still Alive
item.description: EU Parliament voted to end temporary derogation allowing voluntary mass scanning of private messages, but the broader Chat Control proposal for mandatory CSAM detection continues negotiations with focus on age verification.

item.title: EFF Director Cindy Cohn Discusses Privacy Book on The Daily Show
item.description: EFF Executive Director Cindy Cohn appeared on The Daily Show to discuss her new book covering 30 years of digital privacy battles including the Crypto Wars and NSA surveillance.

item.title: EFF Leaves X Platform After 20 Years, Cites Massive Engagement Drop and Policy Failures
item.description: Electronic Frontier Foundation exits X after engagement plummeted 97% since 2018, while staying on other platforms to reach vulnerable communities needing digital rights advocacy.

item.title: EFF Promotes Digital Rights Advocacy and Privacy Education to Daily Show Viewers
item.description: EFF's outreach page highlights 35 years of defending digital rights, from crypto wars to NSA surveillance battles, showcasing their role as infrastructure defenders for online rebellion.

item.title: How Arab Spring Backlash Created Global Surveillance Infrastructure
item.description: EFF analysis shows how governments turned 2011 Arab Spring organizing tools into surveillance infrastructure, creating cybercrime laws and monitoring systems now used globally to suppress dissent.

item.title: South Sudan's New Cybercrime Law Criminalizes Digital Speech with 10-Year Prison Terms
item.description: South Sudan's 2026 Cybercrimes Act criminalizes 'false information' and 'undesirable content' with up to 10 years prison, grants authorities broad surveillance powers, and threatens encrypted communications and journalism.

item.title: Citizen Lab Urges UN to Address Mercenary Spyware Proliferation
item.description: Citizen Lab submitted UN recommendations calling for states to stop outsourcing digital surveillance operations, warning that mercenary spyware threatens democracy and human rights.

item.title: Vietnamese Journalist Gets 8.5 Years for Social Media Posts Critical of State
item.description: Vietnam sentenced journalist Huynh Ngoc Tuan to 8.5 years in prison for Facebook posts and videos deemed anti-state propaganda, highlighting the need for censorship-resistant publishing tools.

item.title: Israeli Pressure Forces Closure of Palestinian Children's Rights Group DCIP
item.description: Defense for Children International-Palestine (DCIP) shut down operations after Israeli military designated it a 'terrorist organization' using secret evidence, raided offices, and confiscated equipment.

item.title: Trump Admin Seeks Medical Records of 8M Federal Workers Through Insurance Companies
item.description: Trump administration quietly proposed requiring insurance companies to hand over detailed medical records of 8+ million federal workers, raising major surveillance and privacy concerns among experts.

item.title: FISA 702 Renewal Could Strip VPN Users of Constitutional Protections
item.description: US surveillance law renewal may classify VPN traffic as 'foreign' by default, potentially subjecting American VPN users to warrantless surveillance under Section 702.

item.title: Hong Kong Democracy Activist Koo Sze-yiu Dies After Decades of Resistance
item.description: Veteran Hong Kong activist Koo Sze-yiu, who protested for over 50 years against both British colonial and Chinese Communist rule, died after continuing resistance despite terminal cancer and Beijing's National Security Law.

item.title: Ugandan Journalist Disappeared After Discussing Struck-Down Digital Censorship Laws
item.description: Arnold Anthony Mukose, journalist and program director at Alternative Digitalk, was forcibly seized by security operatives after broadcasting about a court ruling that nullified Uganda's Computer Misuse Act.

item.title: Galaxy Monitors Bitcoin Network for Iran Oil Tanker Payment Signs
item.description: Galaxy executive Alex Thorn says firm is watching Bitcoin blockchain for evidence of oil tanker fees paid in BTC, potentially indicating sanctions evasion.

item.title: Proton Meet Launches with MLS Encryption to Counter AI Video Surveillance
item.description: Proton launched Meet, an end-to-end encrypted video conferencing service using MLS protocol, addressing AI-powered surveillance risks in mainstream platforms like Zoom and Google Meet.

item.title: US to Begin Automatic Draft Registration, Citing Surveillance Goals
item.description: The Selective Service System will automatically register draft-eligible men starting late 2026, abandoning self-registration in favor of federal database integration for 'surveillance' purposes.

item.title: Russian GRU Compromised 18,000+ Routers for Mass Token Harvesting
item.description: Russian military intelligence (GRU/APT28) hijacked DNS settings on 18,000+ vulnerable routers to intercept Microsoft OAuth tokens from 200+ orgs, bypassing MFA without malware.

item.title: Rwanda Reaffirms Crypto Ban After Bybit Adds Rwandan Franc Support
item.description: Rwanda's central bank swiftly reaffirmed its cryptocurrency ban after Bybit enabled Rwandan franc trading on its P2P platform, highlighting ongoing state resistance to monetary sovereignty bypass attempts.

item.title: Unverified: Iran Allegedly Using Bitcoin for Strait of Hormuz Transit Fees
item.description: Unverified reports claim Iran is charging $1/barrel in Bitcoin for certain ships transiting Strait of Hormuz under supposed US-Iran deal. Lacks credible sourcing.

item.title: EFF Warns UN: Cybercrime Laws and Surveillance Targeting Human Rights Defenders
item.description: EFF submission to UN documents how cybercrime laws, digital surveillance, and platform policies are being weaponized against human rights defenders globally, with insufficient safeguards.

item.title: EFF: States Push DRM-Style 'Print Blocking' Requirements for 3D Printers
item.description: Three U.S. states propose mandating 3D printer manufacturers install algorithmic censorship to block 'gun-like' shapes, with criminal penalties for bypassing the restrictions.

item.title: 2025 Broke Records: 313 Internet Shutdowns Across 52 Countries, Zero Days Without Blackouts
item.description: Access Now reports 2025 had 313 internet shutdowns in 52 countries with no single day free of blackouts. Latin America saw Venezuela continue pattern plus Panama's first shutdowns during social security protests.

item.title: US Government Proposes Rules Forcing AI Companies to Disable Safety Features
item.description: General Services Administration proposes procurement rules requiring contractors to license AI systems for 'all lawful purposes' and disable safety guardrails that might refuse government requests.

item.title: Bellingcat Releases Open-Source Satellite Tool to Counter Iran Internet Blackout
item.description: Bellingcat launched a free damage assessment tool using ESA satellite data to monitor Iran conflict destruction, bypassing commercial imagery restrictions and internet blackouts that limit information flow.

item.title: EFF Analysis: State-Mandated 3D Printer Censorship Will Fail but Harm Users
item.description: EFF explains why proposed state laws requiring "print blockers" on 3D printers won't prevent firearm manufacturing but will create censorship infrastructure that harms legitimate users and manufacturers.

item.title: New Proposal Claims Bitcoin Can Be Quantum-Safe Without Protocol Changes
item.description: Researcher proposes Quantum Safe Bitcoin (QSB) scheme that uses hash-based security instead of elliptic curves to protect against quantum computer attacks without requiring Bitcoin soft forks.

item.title: Georgia Deports Azerbaijani Journalist Despite EU Court Order, Exposes Cross-Border Collusion
item.description: Georgian authorities forcibly deported exiled Azerbaijani journalist Afgan Sadigov in defiance of European Court interim protections, using fabricated charges to circumvent extradition restrictions.

item.title: Hungary Files Espionage Charges Against Journalist Who Exposed Russian Influence Operations
item.description: Hungarian government charged investigative journalist Szabolcs Panyi with espionage after he reported on Russian influence operations, escalating press freedom attacks ahead of elections.

item.title: EFF Warns Against 3D Printer DRM Laws in Three US States
item.description: Three US states propose laws requiring 3D printer manufacturers to install 'print-blocking' software to detect gun-like shapes, effectively mandating DRM that would restrict open-source software and enable vendor surveillance.

item.title: Expert Warns of Privatized Offensive Cyber Operations Under Trump Admin
item.description: Citizen Lab director warns Trump administration may authorize private firms to conduct offensive cyber operations, creating unprecedented risks for oversight, civil liberties, and global cyber security.

item.title: EFF Analysis: How Governments Evolved From Crude Blocking to Sophisticated Censorship Systems
item.description: EFF documents how government censorship evolved from simple website blocks that VPNs could bypass to sophisticated platform control systems, using Egypt's transformation from Arab Spring catalyst to digital dystopia as a case study.

item.title: Street Artist Mear One Connects Bitcoin to Decades of Anti-System Protest Culture
item.description: Veteran LA street artist Mear One discusses parallels between Bitcoin's monetary revolution and four decades of anti-war, anti-financial system graffiti activism in interview ahead of Bitcoin 2026.

item.title: US Media Consolidation Creates Centralized Censorship Infrastructure
item.description: Six corporations now control almost all US media, with the FCC enabling consolidation that creates centralized points for government censorship pressure, illustrated by recent Trump administration editorial compliance.

item.title: Iran: Thousands of Prisoners at Risk Amid Escalating Conflict and Mass Executions
item.description: Human Rights Watch reports thousands of Iranian detainees, including political prisoners and activists, face dual threats from US/Israeli strikes and mass executions by authorities who continue arresting dissidents for documenting attacks.

item.title: Internet Shutdowns Hit Record High: 313 Documented in 2025 Across 52 Countries
item.description: Access Now documents 313 internet shutdowns in 52 countries during 2025, increasingly used to conceal war crimes and human rights abuses, with growing attacks on satellite internet systems.

item.title: CBP Orders $50M Arsenal of Chemical Weapons for Crowd Control
item.description: U.S. Customs and Border Protection plans to purchase up to $50 million in tear gas, projectiles, and other 'less lethal' weapons, including 123 different munition types for potential use against protesters.

item.title: Record 313 Internet Shutdowns in 2025, Eastern Europe/Central Asia Hit 29 Times
item.description: Access Now reports 2025 saw the highest number of internet shutdowns ever recorded globally (313 across 52 countries), with Eastern Europe and Central Asia experiencing 29 shutdowns across 7 countries.

item.title: FAA Issues Nationwide 21-Month Drone Ban Near ICE Vehicles, EFF Challenges
item.description: Trump administration issued unprecedented nationwide flight restriction banning drones within half-mile of ICE/CBP vehicles for 21 months, effectively criminalizing accountability journalism.

item.title: Bangladesh's 2026 Election: 72 Cases of AI-Generated Disinformation Documented
item.description: Study identifies 72 cases of AI-manipulated content during Bangladesh's February 2026 election, including deepfakes of political figures and fabricated media photocards designed to shape electoral outcomes.

item.title: US iPhone Hacking Toolkit 'Coruna' Leaked to Russia, Now Used by Adversaries
item.description: Google researchers exposed 'Coruna,' a sophisticated US government iPhone hacking toolkit that exploits 23 iOS vulnerabilities, reportedly leaked by L3Harris employee to Russia and now used by criminal groups.

item.title: Russians Navigate State Censorship vs Security Trade-offs During Internet Restrictions
item.description: Citizen Lab researcher warns Russians are accepting state-controlled apps over secure alternatives during internet shutdowns, prioritizing immediate access over privacy when seeking critical information.

item.title: New Mexico Court Ruling Uses E2E Encryption Implementation as Evidence Against Meta
item.description: New Mexico court ruling against Meta cited the company's 2023 addition of end-to-end encryption to Messenger as evidence of negligent design, potentially setting precedent that could discourage encryption deployment.

item.title: Hack-for-Hire Campaign Targets Egyptian Journalists with Persistent Spear-Phishing
item.description: Access Now exposes sophisticated hack-for-hire campaign targeting Egyptian journalists Mostafa Al-A'sar and Ahmed Eltantawy through persistent spear-phishing attacks from 2023-2024, likely linked to Asian threat actors.

item.title: Hack-for-Hire Phishing Campaign Targets MENA Civil Society
item.description: Access Now reports a sophisticated phishing campaign targeting civil society activists across the MENA region, part of broader espionage efforts against human rights organizations.

item.title: Egyptian & Lebanese Journalists Hit by State-Linked Spear-Phishing Campaign
item.description: South Asia-based cyber espionage group targeted prominent Egyptian and Lebanese journalists with coordinated spear-phishing attacks against their Apple, Microsoft, and Google accounts, likely commissioned by governments.

item.title: Azerbaijan Supreme Court Upholds 7-9 Year Sentences for Independent Journalists
item.description: Azerbaijan's Supreme Court rejected appeals for 7 journalists from Abzas Media and RFE/RL, upholding lengthy prison sentences for alleged illegal foreign funding in escalating press freedom crackdown.

item.title: Ugandan Journalist Disappeared After Discussing Censorship Law Ruling
item.description: Ugandan security forces forcibly disappeared journalist Mukose Arnold Anthony after he discussed a court ruling that struck down sections of the Computer Misuse Act used to target journalists and activists.

item.title: Google Moves Quantum Cryptography Deadline to 2029, EFF Warns of Encryption 'Y2K Moment'
item.description: Google accelerated post-quantum cryptography deadline to 2029 due to breakthrough research. Current encryption protecting Signal, websites, and secure communications will be vulnerable to quantum attacks.

item.title: MENA Region Hit by 52 Internet Shutdowns Across 15 Countries in 2025
item.description: Access Now reports 313 global internet shutdowns in 2025, with MENA experiencing 52 shutdowns across 15 countries. Authorities increasingly target satellite internet alternatives.

item.title: Global Internet Shutdowns Hit Record High: 313 Blackouts Across 52 Countries in 2025
item.description: Access Now reports 2025 saw 313 internet shutdowns across 52 countries—a record high with no shutdown-free days. Myanmar led with 95 shutdowns, while authorities increasingly target satellite internet systems.

item.title: Mexico Deploys 'Seguritech' Surveillance System in Ciudad Juárez
item.description: Mexican law enforcement is using a surveillance system called 'Seguritech' in Ciudad Juárez, involving map-based tracking interfaces accessed by police officers.

item.title: How Arab Spring Protests Fueled Global Surveillance Infrastructure Boom
item.description: EFF analysis shows how governments turned digital tools that empowered 2011 Arab Spring activists into backbone of global surveillance apparatus, creating permanent monitoring infrastructure now used worldwide.

item.title: EFF Documents Evolution of Digital Censorship Since Arab Spring
item.description: EFF analysis shows how governments transformed from crude internet blocks to sophisticated censorship systems targeting platforms directly, making simple circumvention tools less effective.

item.title: Age Verification Systems Show Mixed Results, Raise Privacy Concerns for All Users
item.description: New research on UK and Australia's age verification laws shows limited effectiveness while creating privacy risks for all internet users, with children easily bypassing restrictions.

item.title: EFF's Cindy Cohn Promotes Privacy Book at DC Events
item.description: EFF Executive Director Cindy Cohn is hosting two DC events to discuss her new book about 30 years of fighting digital surveillance, with proceeds benefiting EFF.

item.title: US Govt Rewrites Procurement Rules to Force AI Companies to Disable Safety Guardrails
item.description: GSA proposes procurement rules requiring AI contractors to license systems for 'all lawful purposes' and disable safety guardrails that might refuse government requests, effectively weaponizing contracts against AI safety.

item.title: Belarus Adopts Vague 'Propaganda' Ban Expanding Censorship Powers
item.description: Belarus parliament passed sweeping law banning 'propaganda' of LGBT relationships and childlessness with deliberately vague definitions, mirroring Russia's playbook for suppressing dissent and controlling discourse.

item.title: Quantum Computing Advances Compress Bitcoin Security Upgrade Timeline
item.description: New research shows quantum computers may need only 10,000-500,000 qubits to break Bitcoin's encryption (down from 10M), accelerating development of quantum-resistant upgrades like BIP-360.

item.title: Durov: Thousands of Developers Building VPNs to Counter State Internet Control
item.description: Telegram CEO Pavel Durov reports thousands of software developers are actively creating VPNs to circumvent government internet restrictions.

item.title: Press Freedom Coalition Challenges Trump's Executive Order Targeting Law Firms
item.description: 42 media organizations led by The Intercept filed amicus brief challenging Trump's executive order sanctioning law firms that represent his political opponents, arguing it undermines press freedom.

item.title: Hong Kong Police Can Now Force Encryption Key Disclosure at Borders
item.description: New Hong Kong law allows police to demand encryption keys for devices, even from airport transit passengers. Refusal is now criminal offense under expanded National Security Law.

item.title: Hack-for-Hire Group Targets Egyptian Journalists with Spear-Phishing Campaign
item.description: Access Now investigation reveals sophisticated spear-phishing attacks targeting Egyptian journalists and government critics, using fake Signal pages and Apple impersonation to deliver Android spyware.

item.title: US Issues Nationwide Drone Ban Near ICE Vehicles to Block Documentation
item.description: The FAA issued a 21-month nationwide restriction criminalizing drone flights within half a mile of any ICE/CBP vehicle, effectively blocking citizen and journalist documentation of immigration enforcement.

item.title: AI Tool Grok Amplifies Online Violence Against Nigerian Women
item.description: X's AI assistant Grok is being weaponized to generate non-consensual sexualized images, systematizing online abuse that already disproportionately targets Nigerian women with weak regulatory oversight.
